title: Today's AI Brief: Claude's new model is more 'honest' when it messes up
summary: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 model prioritizes honesty when making mistakes, while OpenAI's Codex helps build self-improving tax agents.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up
imageCaption: Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I'm looking at some fresh signals from the world of artificial intelligence, and I've got a couple of interesting updates to share with you. It's always exciting to see how AI is evolving, and today I've got two stories that caught my attention.

First up, Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 model is launching on Thursday, and it's being touted as more 'honest' when it makes mistakes. According to The Verge, this new model is designed to be more transparent when it's unsure or doesn't know the answer to a question. I think this is a big deal, because let's face it, we've all been there - stuck with a chatbot that's spouting nonsense. It's like trying to get directions from a friend who's clearly lost. But what's really interesting here is that Anthropic is actively working on making their model more reliable and trustworthy.

Also on the board, OpenAI's Codex is being used to build self-improving tax agents. In a blog post, OpenAI explains how they're working with Thrive and Crete to automate tax filings, improve accuracy, and speed up workflows. My read is that this could be a game-changer for tax season - no more tedious paperwork or software glitches. Just imagine being able to breeze through tax season with minimal hassle. It's a nice thought, right?

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
